description.en: The Canadian lynx is a secretive forest cat with huge, furry paws that act as natural snowshoes.
description.zh: 加拿大猞猁是一种隐秘的森林猫科动物，长着巨大的毛茸茸的爪子，就像天然的雪鞋一样。

anatomy.en: They are recognizable by their silvery-brown coat, distinctive black tufts of hair on the tips of their ears, and a short, stubby tail. Their massive paws spread out to distribute their weight, allowing them to walk effortlessly over deep snow.
anatomy.zh: 它们的特征是银棕色的皮毛、耳朵尖上独特的黑色长毛丛，以及短短的尾巴。它们巨大的脚掌可以张开以分散体重，让它们能在深雪上毫不费力地行走。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Their survival is directly linked to the snowshoe hare. When hare populations boom, lynx numbers go up; when hares disappear, lynx populations crash. This is one of nature's most famous predator-prey cycles!
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 它们的生存与雪鞋兔直接相关。当雪鞋兔数量暴增时，猞猁的数量也会随之增加；当雪鞋兔减少时，猞猁的数量就会锐减。这是自然界最著名的「捕食者与猎物周期」之一！

habitat_and_distribution.en: They roam the dense boreal forests of Canada, Alaska, and parts of the northern United States. They prefer deep, snowy woods where they can easily hunt their favorite prey.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们漫游在加拿大、阿拉斯加以及美国北部部分地区的茂密针叶林中。它们喜欢大雪覆盖的幽深树林，这样可以轻松地捕猎它们最爱的猎物。
